title: NO_DECORRELATE EXISTS subquery does not early-out
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->
```
CREATE TABLE `t1` (
  `a` int,
  `b` int,
  `c` int,
  Primary KEY (`a`),
  KEY `ib` (`b`)
);
set @@cte_max_recursion_depth=10000000;
INSERT INTO t1 (a, b, c)
SELECT a, mod(a, 1000) AS b, mod(a, 10) AS c
FROM (
    WITH RECURSIVE x AS (
        SELECT 1 AS a
        UNION ALL
        SELECT a + 1 AS a
        FROM x
        WHERE a < 1000000
    )
    SELECT a
    FROM x
) AS subquery;
Analyze table t1;
```
Then test the following SQLs - where adding LIMIT 1 will simulate early-out.
Note - column a is PK. column b is indexed. column c is not indexed. Each example has one SQL without limit, and one with limit.
```
explain analyze select a, if(exists(select /*+ NO_DECORRELATE() */ 1 from t2 t2 where t2.b = t1.b),1,0) from t2 t1 where c < 2;
explain analyze select a, if(exists(select /*+ NO_DECORRELATE() */ 1 from t2 t2 where t2.b = t1.b limit 1),1,0) from t2 t1 where c < 2;
explain analyze select a, if(exists(select /*+ NO_DECORRELATE() */ 1 from t2 t2 where t2.c = t1.c),1,0) from t2 t1 where c < 2;
explain analyze select a, if(exists(select /*+ NO_DECORRELATE() */ 1 from t2 t2 where t2.c = t1.c limit 1),1,0) from t2 t1 where c < 2;
```
### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

Would expect to see similar performance with and without LIMIT 1.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

Correlated on b
without LIMIT == 2 min 50 sec
with LIMIT == approx 600 millisec

Correlated on c
without LIMIT == 940 millisec
with LIMIT == approx 350 millisec

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->

master branch
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (id INTEGER, b INTEGER, c VARCHAR(10), PRIMARY KEY (id), INDEX ib (b));
INSERT INTO t2 (id, b, c) VALUES (1, 100, 'test1'), (2, 200, 'test2'), (3, 300, 'test3');
SELECT a, CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM t2 t2 WHERE t2.b = t1.b) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM t1 WHERE c < 5;
SELECT a, CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM t2 t2 WHERE t2.b = t1.b LIMIT 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM t1 WHERE c < 5;
SELECT a, CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM t2 t2 WHERE t2.c = 'test1') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM t1 WHERE c < 5;
key_bug_features: NO_DECORRELATE() subquery does not early-out with LIMIT
